description_long: This object is a karambit-style knife with a pronounced, curved blade and a textured, black handle. The blade is single-edged, tapering to a sharp point, and features a matte, slightly rough finish. The handle is ergonomically contoured with finger grooves and a prominent finger ring at the end, providing a secure grip. The entire knife appears to be constructed from metal, possibly steel, with a dark coating or finish for added grip and reduced reflectivity. There are no visible decorative elements or engravings, giving it a utilitarian and tactical appearance. The knife is compact, lightweight, and designed for precise, controlled use.
description: A compact, black karambit knife with a curved blade and textured ergonomic handle featuring a finger ring. The design is tactical and utilitarian, with a matte, non-reflective finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved blade, finger grooves, and finger ring on the handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and handle, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the curved blade and ergonomic handle with finger ring.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, emphasizing the blade's thin profile.
attribute_handle_design: contoured, textured black handle with finger grooves and a finger ring
